message.content: {"items":[{"title":"ISSP 1985: Role of Government I (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13540","justification":"Includes Finnish data on attitudes towards economic roles of government and welfare-state interventions; while not limited to the 1960s, it provides retrospective and contextual information on economic systems and development relevant for post‑1960s comparisons."},{"title":"Finnish Election Study 1966","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=0123","justification":"Study on the 1966 parliamentary election in Finland; includes variables on economic policy preferences, perceptions of economic conditions, and party positions on economic development in the 1960s."},{"title":"Finnish Election Study 1970 (includes retrospective questions on the 1960s)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=0124","justification":"Focuses on the 1970 parliamentary election in Finland but contains retrospective items on economic conditions and political conflict over economic development during the late 1960s."},{"title":"International Social Justice Project 1991 (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10202","justification":"Contains Finnish data on perceptions of economic inequality, distributive justice, and evaluations of economic systems; includes retrospective assessments of economic development relevant to understanding the legacy of the 1960s economic model."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 3 (1970-1971) – Economic and Social Development, Finland Sample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12220","justification":"Includes Finnish respondents and focuses on attitudes towards economic and social development in the early 1970s, with questions referencing economic change and development paths begun in the 1960s."},{"title":"World Values Survey 1981–1984 (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12224","justification":"Finnish subsample includes questions about views on economic systems, inequality, and development; many items invite respondents to evaluate societal development over previous decades, including the 1960s."},{"title":"ISSP 1990: Social Inequality I (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13543","justification":"Contains Finnish data on perceived fairness of income distribution, social structure, and evaluations of economic arrangements, with items that reference long‑term development and can be used to study the outcomes of Finland’s 1960s economic system."},{"title":"ISSP 1992: Family and Changing Gender Roles II (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13545","justification":"Finnish data on gender roles intersects with economic development, labour-market participation, and welfare‑state expansion that began in the 1960s, useful for studying socio‑economic system changes originating in that decade."},{"title":"ISSP 1996: Role of Government III (Finland)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13549","justification":"Provides Finnish opinions on economic regulation, redistribution, and state involvement in the economy; questions about perceived changes and evaluations of government roles can be linked back to institutions and development trajectories established in the 1960s."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 6 (1976) – Economic Conditions and Social Change, Finland Sample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12223","justification":"Includes Finnish respondents and items on economic conditions, inflation, unemployment, and evaluations of economic policy; captures public assessment of the economic system and development path largely shaped during the 1960s."}]}
